blocks:
Ayahs

إِذَا زُلْزِلَتِ ٱلْأَرْضُ زِلْزَالَهَا (۱)
When the earth is rocked with a terrible quake (1)
وَأَخْرَجَتِ ٱلْأَرْضُ أَثْقَالَهَا (۲)
and discharges its burdens, (2)
وَقَالَ ٱلْإِنسَـٰنُ مَا لَهَا (۳)
and man says, ‘What is the matter with her?’ (3)
يَوْمَئِذٍ تُحَدِّثُ أَخْبَارَهَا (٤)
On that day she will relate her chronicles (4)
بِأَنَّ رَبَّكَ أَوْحَىٰ لَهَا (٥)
for her Lord will have inspired her. (5)
يَوْمَئِذٍ يَصْدُرُ ٱلنَّاسُ أَشْتَاتًا لِّيُرَوْا۟ أَعْمَـٰلَهُمْ (٦)
On that day, mankind will issue forth in various groups to be shown their deeds. (6)
فَمَن يَعْمَلْ مِثْقَالَ ذَرَّةٍ خَيْرًا يَرَهُۥ (۷)
So whoever does an atom’s weight of good will see it, (7)
وَمَن يَعْمَلْ مِثْقَالَ ذَرَّةٍ شَرًّا يَرَهُۥ (۸)
and whoever does an atom’s weight of evil will see it. (8)

Key Points

The meaning of «اثقالها» (its burdens) is the dead, whom the earth casts out on the Day of Judgment. As He states in another Ayah: «و القت ما فیها و تخلّت» (105) The presence of people on the Day of Judgment will be scattered. As He states in other Ayahs: «اشتاتاً», «كانّهم جراد منتشر» (106), «كالفراش المبثوث» (107) The scattered presence of people on the Day of Judgment is either because everyone will be accompanied by the leader they chose in this world, or because the good and the wicked will be separated from one another. By the will of Allah on the Day of Judgment, even inanimate objects will speak, not just the earth, but the limbs of the human body will testify against him. «و قالوا لجلودهم لم شهدتم علینا قالوا انطقنا اللّه الّذى انطق كلّ شى ء» (108) It is mentioned in narrations that you should pray in different places, because that place will bear witness for you on the Day of Judgment. (109) In another narration, it is stated that you should also call the Adhan (call to prayer) in the deserts, because every stone and clod of earth that hears your voice will be a witness for you on the Day of Judgment. After distributing the public treasury (Bayt al-Mal), Hadrat Ali عليه السلام would perform two units of prayer and address the earth, saying: «اشهدى انّى ملئتك بحقّ و فرغتك بحق» (110) O Earth, bear witness that I filled you with the public treasury based on truth (justice) and then emptied you based on truth (justice). It is mentioned in narrations that the way the earth reports is by saying: So-and-so performed such-and-such an action upon me at such-and-such a time. (111) «Mithqal» (weight) is derived from «Thaqil» (heavy) and means a measure of weight. The word «Dharrah» (atom/particle) means either the smallest ant or the light particles scattered in the air. Of course, today the atom is also called Dharrah. But in any case, what is meant by it is the smallest unit of weight. We read in narrations: An Arab man entered the mosque of the Holy Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله and said: «علّمنى بما علمك اللّه» (Teach me what Allah has taught you!) The Prophet assigned someone to teach him the Quran. The teacher read Surah Idha Zulzilat... for him. As soon as he reached the Ayah «Faman ya’mal mithqal...», the Arab man said: «كفانى» (That is enough for me) and then left. The Prophet said: «رجع فقیهاً» (He returned as a jurist/man of understanding). (112) Also, in Surah Luqman, Luqman tells his son: If you have a good or bad deed equal to the weight of a mustard seed, whether it is inside a rock, or in the heights of the heavens, or in the earth, Allah will bring it forth for reckoning on the Day of Judgment. «یا بنىّ انّها اِن تك مثقال حبّة من خردل فتكن فى صخرة او فى السموات او فى الارض یأت بها اللّه» (113) The testimony and witnessing of the earth on the Day of Judgment indicates that, even now, the earth consciously perceives our actions, and on that Day, it will recount them by divine command and revelation. Mr. Hassan, in his collection of poems (Diwan), has rendered the verses of Surah Zilzal into poetry as follows:

This earth, in that time full of calamity, Suddenly, when zulzilat zilzaluha (it is shaken with its [final] shaking) From within it, akhrajat athqalaha (it casts out its burdens), In astonishment, qala al-insanu ma laha (man says, 'What is wrong with it?') The dead rise up, all of them, So that all people yaraw a’malahum (may see their deeds) Whoever brings dharratan khayran yarah (an atom's weight of good, shall see it), Or whoever brings dharratan sharran yarah (an atom's weight of evil, shall see it). At that time, the shining sun kuwwirat (is wrapped up), The hard and heavy mountains suyyirat (are set in motion). The waters in the mouth of the sea sujjirat (are set ablaze), The fire of Hell is intensely su’irat (kindled). Since at that time Paradise uzlifat (is brought near), Everyone will know ma ahdarat (what he has brought forth).

The attribution of the earthquake to the earth («zilzaluha») is both a symbol of the earthquake's magnitude and a symbol of its widespread nature across the entire earth. The magnitude of this earthquake is also mentioned in the first Ayah of Surah Al-Hajj: «انّ زَلزلة الساعة شى ءٌ عظیم» We also read in Sermon 157 of Nahj al-Balaghah: «عباد اللّه احذروا یوما تفحص فیه الاعمال و یكثر فیه الزلزال و تشیب فیه الاطفال» O servants of Allah! Beware of a Day when all deeds shall be examined, earthquakes shall increase, and children shall turn gray (due to the length or severity of that Day).

105) Al-Inshiqaq, 4. 106) Al-Qamar, 7. 107) Al-Qari’ah, 4. 108) Fussilat, 21. 109) Tafsir Majma‘ al-Bayan. 110) La’ali al-Akhbar, Vol. 5, p. 79. 111) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn. 112) Tafasir Namuneh and Nur al-Thaqalayn. 113) Luqman, 16.

Messages

1- The great earthquake of the earth preceding the Day of Judgment is a definite and certain matter. «اذا زلزلت الارض زلزالها» (The word idha [when] is used in cases where the matter is certain.)

2- The Resurrection is physical. (The bodies of humans are buried in the earth and will be brought out on the Day of Judgment, not their souls.) «اخرجت الارض اثقالها»

3- The Day of Judgment is a day of human bewilderment. «قال الانسان ما لها»

4- Existence possesses consciousness. «یومئذ تحدث اخبارها»

5- The earth is among the witnesses on the Day of Judgment. «یومئذ تحدّث اخبارها»

6- On the Day of Judgment, the path to any denial is closed. «یروا اعمالهم»

7- All people are equal in the court of Divine Justice. «فمن یعمل... فمن یعمل...»

8- Divine recompense and reward are based on action. «فمن یعمل... فمن یعمل...»

9- No matter how small an action is, it will be accounted for. Therefore, do not belittle sins, nor acts of worship. «مثقال ذرّة»

10- The embodiment and viewing of the deed on that Day is itself a torment or a pleasure. «یره»
